name: Mission 12 - This Is Nightlight, Signing Off

text.content:
There's one last thing Rogue and her backers need to really get the Afterlife spinning - and she's been broadcasting across Night City every night from 11 to 1.

This Is Nightlight, Signing Off

Friend Opportunity: Nightlight

Nightlight is a true-blue edgerunner in her own way, and a genuine smooth operator. She'll rub elbows with anyone, but the only people she calls friends are those same folks as her, who utterly ooze edgerunning spirit. And she isn't afraid to boost their rep, either.

Enemy Opportunity - Militech Or Maelstrom Leader

Two of Night City's biggest groups of badasses are trading blows in the ruins of the Old City Center - the commander of either would make for a suitable player enemy.

Rep Opportunity: This Is Nightlight, Broadcasting Live

Get Nightlight on the air while you're rescuing her. If this achievement is passed, every party member present to benefit from the coverage gains a personal rep.

Gig Payout

5 Eagles + 300eb/player upon successful recovery of Nightlight, a rep boost for finishing the campaign, and a couple days of free Afterlife services in celebration of the final services being unlocked.

Beat Chart

Beat 1 - Rogue Signals

Rogue's away on business; Vido rears his head to offer the players their final job in the Afterlife's startup, and to congratulate them on flying so high.

Beat 2 - Dead Air

Nightlight's broadcast clues as to her location lead to the ruins of the Old City Center, an irradiated mess of concrete, steel, Maelstrom and Militech. Secure the site.

Beat 3 - Pirate Wavelength

Having located Nightlight's HQ, the team is confronted with a strange mystery that won't be around for long.

Beat 4 - Guerilla Radio

Time to get Nightlight out, but be careful, you're not the only ones interested in doing so.

Beat 5 - Final Festivities

Back at the Afterlife, a party is being thrown at least in part in the team's honor.

The FM Receiver Crackles, The Pirate Wavelength Carrying A Staticky, Stuttering Female Voice

"G-g-g-ood even-n-n-ing Night C-c-c-ity, this is N-N-Nightlight, comin -#$%$#- li-v-v-e from the l-l-last -#$%$#- you'd ever l-l-l-ook. I hope y-y-you're -#$%$#- and ready, t-t-time to party like i-i-it's #$-%$#- of y-y-your life.

"Our t-t-top story t-t-toni -#$%$#- in Upper Marina, e-e-everyone who's a-a-anyone will -#$%$#- i-i-including yours truly. S-s-special -#$%$#- group of r-r-regular all star-r-rs who've m-m-made it possible, -#$%$#- who you are.

"In other-r-r news, looks -$%$#- in the O-O-Old City Cent-t-ter last n-n-night, -#$%$#- lights out-t-t for the scav-v-vs living there and-d-d their captives. Wor-r-rd is -#$%$#- t-t-the area, as well as M-M-Militech and even rumors of -#$%$#-. No one's t-t-taken responsibility yet-t-t, but -#$%$#- glory se-e-ekers -#$%$#- it's only-y-y a matter of t-t-time.

"Als-s-so hearing report-t-ts -#$%$#- re-re-reclaiming efforts hav-v-ve doubled acros-s-s the city s-s-since -#$%$#- major re-e-e-wards for the all-ll-ll stars making it happen, so the next t-t-time you grab a n-n-new Agen-t-t-t at -#$%$#- thank-k-ks they deser-#$%$#-

"Up ne-

-#$%$#-

This beat takes place in the @Scene[fqgvewCqt8z94mll]{The 7th Circle Bar & Casino + Holodeck}. To begin:

  • Place the player tokens near the entrance.

  • Place @Actor[17CTAE5lKmZFHY9i]{Vido} in his usual booth at the back, @Actor[rQdrdTOYV13yilZD]{Sammy} at the bar, @Actor[PJBDw6Qy61QZGIXp]{Silvers} at the door, and @Actor[Y7NnvbMut6swzVaA]{Checkers} in the pit.

  • Populate the club at your discretion - it's busier than usual tonight.

A Call For Celebration

Your agent pings - a text from Vido. The first one in awhile!

"Bonjour, mis vouchers! Originally, I was to invite you to an event this evening as the guests of honor, in celebration of the Afterlife's successes - but it appears that was not to be! Report to me at your earliest convenience in the 7th Circle, and we shall do what needs done. Do it quickly, and perhaps you will be finished in time for the celebrations!"

Later, downstairs, in the 7th Circle, the attitude is festive. The Afterlife is fully operational, which is no mean feat. The digital fires on the club floor are brighter and multicolored; there are drink specials at the bar, and decorations are hung around the club. A combat tournament is being planned in the danger room, and high-stakes poker is planned down in the pit. Even despite the preparations, the club is occupied as ever. The Afterlife rarely sleeps, nowadays, and something small like prep for the party of the year isn't about to change that.

Vido waves you over, the thin vineer of cigarette smoke hanging over his booth in the back, and begins the briefing.

Mission Details

"First, I must apologize! The Owner wanted to be here personally, but she and much of our security forces are otherwise occupied, and I was not privy to the details of the engagement. But nonetheless, business must continue! You are aware of Nightlight, yes? The pirate broadcaster responsible for much of the news which circulates in our little community. If you have been listening, you will have heard of her… predicament."

Vido pauses, allowing the recording that has been playing over Nightlight's pirate frequency to loop. ((see @UUID[.p32p3gKrHWYWoWOT]{Nightlight... Editorial?}))

"We have ourselves the unique sort of Night City situation which only a crew such as yourselves are positioned to solve. Here are the details."

Nightlight has been a mystery to the Afterlife and its staff for some time. In theory, the Owner would have liked to recruit her, but all they could ever find was her former name and a string of old hideouts that had long-since been dispossessed.

  • Nightlight's real name was Nora Jackson, a young media of little report who grew up as a NC local in Heywood during the 2020s and 30s - very little is known about her otherwise.

    • Among many other things, the DataKrash wiped medical records and birth certificates, and if Nora's parents are still alive, they never came forward during the Afterlife's investigation.

    • Her media work was small, but she obviously had talent and was being noticed by the right people - until about 6 months prior to the campaign start, a few months after she'd begun her broadcasts, when the accuracy of her reporting and a seeming clairvoyance surrounding certain headlines shot her popularity through the roof.

About 8 months before the start of Ticket To The Afterlife, she disappeared without a trace - except, of course, that she was still broadcasting.

  • The broadcasts are bounced through transponders across the city, making them virtually impossible to track down, and the presence of heavy redundancies and self-monitoring systems suggests that Nora is a talented netrunner as well as media.

As of last night, it appears Nightlight is in trouble, judging from her broadcast. Vido and the Owner believe that the broadcast was a coded message for help from the Afterlife.

  • Aside from the static and distortion, Nightlight's broadcasts always follow a pattern with certain hallmarks - every broadcast has a certain signon, an order to the news, and so on, which this broadcast broke.

    • Additionally, Nora mentioned that she'll be at the Afterlife in person - and, in fact, has been there before - but none of the Afterlife staff have any knowledge of her having ever entered the establishment, in alias or otherwise.

  • The broadcast contains clues that Vido wants the party to follow up on - a raid in the Old City Center.

    • Vido's sourced some basic details on the raid - a black ops team presumed to belong to Militech raided a scav stronghold, wiped them out, then were driven out by Maelstrom, who now hold the building.

    • Vido doesn't know what Militech wants in the building, or why the raid was last night in particular, but assumes that the corp was after Nightlight, for some reason.

      • He also doesn't know why Maelstrom decided to attack Militech, but assumes that it's not exactly intelligent design - a black ops team was on their turf and obviously wants something, which means Maelstrom wants it too.

  • Vido also suspects that Nora requested the party in particular, since they're the "group of all stars" she mentioned in her broadcast, a title she also used to refer to the "reclaimers," the scavengers across the city and North America which specialize in diving ruins to recover rare and valuable salvage.

He wants the party to go investigate the ruins and, if Nightlight is there, to recover her and return her safely to the Afterlife. If it turns out to be a bunk lead or the party doesn't feel like they're capable of facing off against Militech and/or Maelstrom in force, he notes that he'll still pay half the listed amount (2 Eagles + 150eb) per edgerunner for the attempt.

Once players are ready to move in, proceed to beat 2.

For your convenience in ad-libbing, here is the sequence of events as they played out prior to the players investigating during the gig:

  • At some point in the past (more than 6 months/before the start of the campaign), Nightlight was an up-and-coming media and netrunner who decided to hijack a pirate frequency and begin broadcasting to Night City's slowly rebuilding edgerunning community.

    • She saw moderate, but not extraordinary, success, mostly due to her alluring voice, her commitment to the truth, and her fearlessness in her reporting.

  • Eventually she made a powerful enemy in Netwatch, as she was experimenting with tapping the Old Net to dig into modern systems ("digging in through the basement") to search out scoops before they appeared and confirm otherwise impossible headlines.

    • In so doing, she discovered a 'friend', an erratic AI personality construct which followed her back as a fugitive from the newly-constructed Blackwall and installed itself into her chipware.

    • This symbiosis boosted her intelligence and netrunning abilities, but also created a psychosis, as she found herself increasingly addicted to the net.

    • The personality was initially propelled purely by a hate of the corporate world, but over time, grew to also enjoy the sensations of Nightlight's life, living as it was in her head.

  • Nightlight interfacing with the AI and increasingly deeper dives required hardware only available in old-world ruins, and she was also running out of places to hide from Netwatch and their Militech black-ops partners.

    • Thanks to her AI's guidance, this led her to a bunker in the Old Combat Zone where she was able to hide, and regular payments to scavs and/or Maelstrom ensured shipments of food and other essentials.

  • Eventually a run went bad - the Blackwall itself killed Nightlight, sensing the AI in her cerebrum.

    • As she died, Nightlight ejected a storage chip from her slots, which contains an isolated, dormant version of the AI that was living in her head.

    • The version of the AI still connected to the Net escaped the clutches of the Blackwall thanks to Nora's death, but felt terribly guilty about this, having been humanized through long-term exposure to Nightlight.

      • Already partially modeling itself after the original Nora's personality, the AI decided to carry on her work to atone for getting her killed.

  • With the AI taking direct control, it/she no longer had any limitations in its mission except to avoid attracting Netwatch to its/her hideaway beneath the Old City Center.

    • Nightlight's popularity and accuracy skyrocketed as a result - the AI tapped into everything from corporate systems to traffic cameras, seeming to know about headlines before they even occurred.

    • It's during this era that the campaign presumably starts - around 3-6 months after the death of the original Nightlight.

  • Over the course of the campaign, AI-Nightlight continued her reporting, but Netwatch was hot on her tail, slowly zeroing in on her position after each broadcast.

    • The night before the gig begins, a Militech black ops unit conducts a raid on the scav den in the Old City Center which is directly above the bunker Nightlight was broadcasting from, damaging her transmission commitment and isolating the bunker in the process.

    • When the AI realized she was under attack, she retreated hurriedly from the NET, attracting the attention of the Blackwall in the process.

    • The ensuing fight (physical and digital) destroys her transmission equipment and isolates her servers from the Net - along with a fragment of the Blackwall, which, cut off from the net on unfamiliar territory, AI-Nightlight manages to fend off at great cost to herself, corrupting both her own program and the Blackwall's code fragment in the process.

    • Before Militech could finish the job, they were attacked and pushed back by Maelstrom, who are currently battling to hold the building - whatever Militech wants, they know it'll be valuable, and want it for themselves.

    • The AI, meanwhile, has sent out a disguised call for help to the Afterlife as her heavily-distorted nightly broadcast, lacking any other means of contacting them.

      • She/it intends to seek refuge at the Afterlife, offering her services in exchange for safe harbor from Netwatch - a long shot, but better odds than anything else she/it can muster.

This beat takes place in the @UUID[Scene.374bQGpfwqtgOt2A]{Old City Center Ruins} scene and uses the @UUID[Scene.H5yxGI7jxQtTxJCM]{Old City Center} environment. To begin:

  • Place a hardened gang of Maelstrom mooks in the boulding on the south side of the street, considering of:

    • 2/3 Partycount (rounded down) @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.4xU3a21yd0HN77KT]{Maelstrom Brawlers}

    • 2/3 Partycount (rounded down) @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.HVnFvv61CdCL6vcZ]{Maelstrom Punishers}

    • 2 Maelstrom Rippers (either variant)

    • 1 Maelstrom Psycho

  • Place a black ops Militech strike team on the building to the north side of the street, consisting of:

    • 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.nETJMfdVTY72YwJa]{Militech Strike Commander} equipped with their sword loadout

    • 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.kkITSSkpW4W9dAOx]{Militech Specialist} equipped with their sniper loadout.

    • 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.xco99o2zPkLF4jeX]{Militech Brute}.

    • If your party numbers 4 or more, they are also accompanied by 3 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.6rEW0UHcCQVMShPe]{Militech Suppressors}

    • If your party numbers 6 or more, they are also accompanied by 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.w3zuHZSRIUm6okoI]{Militech Demolitionist}.

  • Place 2 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.t89AW0hfFOpHHaHw]{RatSec Security Turrets} next to the door on the southeast side of the map, both with half their health and armor SP remaining.

    • These are Nightlight's - they're inoperable when the scene begins, but will spring to life when Nightlight sees the party attempting to access the vault, and attack anyone who isn't a party member.

  • Place the player tokens on the west side of the map, in the ruined building there.

  • Switch to the environment

Then read the following:

50,000 People Used To Live Here, Now It's A Ghost Town…

Even after 20 years, the Old City Center is still in informal quarantine. After the bombing of Arasaka Tower turned half the city's landfill foundations to quicksand, roads crumbled, buildings toppled and seawater rushed in, making a desolate, post-apocalyptic landscape. Some of it was cleared away and tossed into the bay as more fill to build on. Most of it wasn't.

It wasn't a big bomb that hit Saka tower, and the radiation and toxicity that continues to hover over the area is only partially attributed to it. Other bits are attributed to the dozens of other hazardous experiments and toxic sites that were cracked open like rotten eggs when everything fell apart. A popular conspiracy is that Maelstrom is also polluting the area themselves, wanting to keep their hidden HQ secure and discourage scavs and the homeless from moving in to contest the territory. Nobody's bothered investigating to find out if that's true, though.

The roads get bumpy as you get close, and before long, a 20 foot drop into an exposed sewer line means that your ride can't get any further. Just as well that the combat cabs don't run any deeper than this anyway - from here, it's on foot.

Navigating the ruins of the city center is a treacherous endeavor, and there's a reason that even the boostergangs avoid it - while navigating to the ruins on foot, each member of the party must make the following checks:

  • A DV17 Resist Drugs/Torture check to avoid any negative effects of the fallout, rancid chemicals, and toxic biohazards. The difficulty of this check can be reduced by 2 if the user is wearing an @UUID[Compendium.cyberpunk-red-core.core_gear.LTxStSIeRegbtZpd]{Anti-Smog Breathing Mask} and/or has @UUID[Compendium.cyberpunk-red-core.core_cyberware.OhW1h2rwb7lvJPs3]{Toxin Binders} installed.

    • If the check is failed, the party member becomes fatigued. If failed multiple times, each subsequent failure results in 2d6 damage inflicted directly to their HP (this damage cannot inflict critical injuries).

    • Each party member must reattempt this check every hour they're in the area, or every 2 hours if one of the above items is in use. Note that the mask replaces head armor if worn.

  • A DV19 Athletics check to navigate the ruins. The difficulty of this check can be reduced by 2 if the user uses a @UUID[Compendium.cyberpunk-red-core.core_gear.VrwVUEHuMCGjp8Fi]{Grapple Gun} or @UUID[Compendium.cyberpunk-red-core.core_cyberware.v69jYqL7RMbs9PW4]{Grapple Hand}, and by 2 more if the user has paired @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsItems22.se4PZ6IevRDLLn67]{Grip Feet}.

    • On failure, the user takes 1d6 damage for every 2 DV the check was failed by, to a max of 5d6. This damage is ablated by armor as normal, and can cause critical injuries.

Once every member of the party has made both checks, change the scene to Old City Center Ruins, and read the following:

Fires are still burning at the ruins of the building Nightlight was talking about in her broadcast. It looks like a parking structure of some kind, unearthed when the building atop it toppled, and raised to the light by the shifting terrain. A section of broken asphalt provides a narrow access between it and the cracked, burned out office building opposite, and you can see movement from both buildings, and corpses on the street. It's obvious that the blows being traded between Militech and Maelstrom haven't subsided just yet, as the periodic shots between one building and the other would indicate.

Time to break the tie.

Breaking The Tie

Maelstrom and Militech will not immediately reengage when the players enter the scene, though both are hostile to the players - Militech due to a kill on sight order (+ the fact that they're not supposed to be within Night City limits at all) and Maelstrom because… Maelstrom.

Instead, attacking one will cause the other to engage, targeting both opposing groups in the process.

  • Maelstrom will fight to the death - Militech, meanwhile, will retreat if a third or more of the strike team is brought below half health, or if a quarter of their number are critically injured.

Scouting can reveal the enemy numbers and locations.

  • This can be done with a DV17 Conceal/Reveal object or DV21 Perception check for each respective gang - the player making the check may add +3 for using a sniper scope or another source of magnification, or by getting dangerously close to the building they're scouting (GM discretion).

The party's goal is not to defeat both camps, but get into the sealed bunker beneath the Maelstrom building. This door, which is located on the southeast side of the map, can be opened with a DV19 Electronics Security check (the door interface is damaged), or a DV11 Interface check. The door can be closed behind them, though the party's entrance will have compromised the security systems which were formerly stopping Maelstrom, meaning that it won't stay sealed for long.

When the door is accessed, Nightlight's security turrets will spring to life and help defend any party member attempting to access the door.

Once players are inside the bunker, proceed to Beat 3.

Battle through Nightlight's blind automated defenses in time with survivors from Maelstrom/Militech… and find her corpse.

The acrid, nostril-singing scent of the Old City Center hazards finally, mercifully, fades, but is almost immediately replaced by the mustiness of stale filters and old decay. The air is clear, but the corridor before you is dark, periodically illuminated only by flashing red warning lights - whether an alarm was triggered when you breached the door, or if it's been on since who-knows-when. At the opposite end of the short hallway, double glass doors are shut, but open on your approach.

Inside is what you'd expect from a corporate doomsday bunker. It's small, single-room, with a kitchen and a bathroom. It's modern by 2020s standards, though obviously predates the fall of the tower - the floor has cracked from shifting in places, and there's thin piles of dust and debris in the corners from similar cracks in the walls and ceiling.

It stinks worse on the other side of the door, with the sweet scent of mildew and decay. On the other side of the room is the source of the smell - the corpse of a woman, lying in a netrunning chair, her remains desiccated and rotting, leeching into the upholstery. It's obvious she's been here awhile.

A DV15 First Aid or Paramedic check is sufficient to determine that the corpse is of an African American woman.

  • It appears her head caught fire, and given the ruins of the electronics at the base of her skull, it's not a major leap to assume she was netrunning and something went wrong.

A DV17 Paramedic or DV21 First Aid check is sufficient to determine the time of death, revealing that the woman has been dead for more than 6 months, but less than a year.

A DV17 Conceal/Reveal object check also reveals a small chip that Nightlight appears to have taken out of her skull as her dying action, clenched in her closed fist or lying on the floor nearby.

Behind the body is another set of glass doors - behind them, a console is hooked up to a server rack, all of which are still powered. An access point, presumably not just into the net, but the old net. Why it's here or how it's still powered are mysteries for another day, as the console is active, an old-net browser window pulled up.

I have no mouth…

When a player approaches the console, an AI version of Nightlight begins opening browser windows, as follows, revealing what happened here:

(note: the Nightlight on the console/servers is heavily corrupted, unable to speak and trapped on the hardware with a fragment of the Blackwall - hence the cryptic transmission and the communication through web articles instead of… anything else.)

  • Lights Out!: A local screamsheet in Heywood from about a year and a half ago discusses the disappearance of Nightlight, his friend and co-reporter, following violence from the gangs and corps she was reporting on.

    • The article is framed as a call to action to the NCPD and Night Corp to restrain the city's major players and enforce the freedom of the press that they're supposed to protect.

  • When The World Ends: An expose about the many ruins buried beneath Night City, with a map of various doomsday bunkers that have been abandoned and uncovered.

  • Blackwall Boogeyman: A news article from Network 54 highlighting the implementation of a new program (circa 2 years ago) called the Blackwall and how it will finally end the permanent recession of the Red Decades.

  • Ghosts In The Machine: A biography of well known netrunners that were destroyed by Soulkiller or other rogue AIs during the 2020s, and a more recent addendum noting that the new Blackwall seems similarly indiscriminate in executing runners.

  • Digitize Me, Captain: A collection of reports surrounding the controversy of VI constructs being left to manage family affairs after the person those constructs are based on has passed away.

  • All New, All Fit, All Fat: A popup ad for a new, universally-compatible line of storage drives, asking the user to buy now.

The Nightlight on the machine is heavily damaged and doesn't understand her surroundings, which is all that's spared her from being totally destroyed by the also-damaged Blackwall fragment and also helps explain the cryptic broadcast.

  • If the players deduce that an AI is on the machine and attempt to download it, they can do so - but the Blackwall fragment comes with it, permanently corrupting the storage device used except for, again, the Nightlight fragment.

  • Not much changes from machine to machine except that the following check can only be attempted once, once Nightlight is disconnected from the servers.

Nightlight cannot be copied or her programming tampered with except to defrag her personality matrix.

  • Doing so requires a DV15 Interface check or DV24 Electronics/Security

    • On failure, the target immediately takes 4d6 damage direct to HP (Interface) or one of the servers in the room explodes to deal 3d6 damage in a 4m radius (Electronics/Security).

    • Once all the server racks have exploded (there are 5), the console ceases functioning altogether - the copy of Nightlight which has been telling the news for the last 6 months dies, along with the fragment of the Blackwall.

  • If the defrag is successful, Nightlight regains her bearings and sends a message to the players via radio, starting Beat 4.

The defrag check can be circumvented by plugging Nora's old brain chip into the console, reuniting the two Nightlight fragments and destroying the Blackwall remnant on the system.

Once 30 minutes have passed or Nightlight has been defragged, proceed to beat 4.

This beat is a continuation of Beat 3, but requires setup of either Militech or Maelstrom reinforcements. If both Militech and Maelstrom were totally wiped out during Beat 2, the party can leave the bunker without issue, safely skipping this beat.

If Militech survived -

  • Place any surviving members of the Militech squad in defensive positions around the bunker.

  • Place a @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.OtEZqK6hA9CVIBAe]{Militech A/V-4} somewhere in the scene, hovering overhead to provide fire support.

  • Add 2 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Fn6sba31t3fGJBOM]{Militech Grunts} and 2 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.6rEW0UHcCQVMShPe]{Militech Suppressors} to the scene, either just outside the bunker doors or creeping into the bunker to ambush the party.

If Maelstrom survived -

  • Place any surviving members of Maelstrom outside the bunker doors.

  • These are accompanied by an additional @Actor[zL4MIuNuqr9QOANV]{Maelstrom Psycho}, representing gang leadership, as well as 1/2 partycount @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.zeG9k6liLkdHNrf4]{Booster Gangsters} that the psycho dragged along as meat shields.

  • At GM discretion, you can also place up to 1/2 partycount Maelstrom Brawlers or Punishers on the scene for added difficulty.

Then, assuming the beat isn't being skipped, read the following…

Lights Out (Militech Version)

In the 2020s, a bunker like this likely would've been considered nigh-impregnable, or at least, incursion-resistant. Especially if it had a corporate HQ on its head. But it's not the 2020s, and the entire room shakes as an explosion at the front doors leaves ears ringing.

In the hall, dark shapes are moving amid the concrete dust, and more such debris is piling down into the room from the cracked ceiling - a couple more explosions like that one, and the bunker will be buried under its own weight. As the shapes advance, though, you can hear military chatter: "Breach successful - sterilization team, moving in."

More Militech, apparently, and they don't sound too interested in negotiating.

Roll for initiative.

Militech has been employed by Netwatch to track down AI Nora and either destroy her, or recover her for integration into the Blackwall.

  • If Nora starts broadcasting, the team will pursue the party until they disappear into the ruins, and continue patrolling in their A/V-4 after that point.

    • Eluding their grasp requires a DV17 Stealth check from each party member - they can make the check up to 3 times, but only need to pass once.

      • Each time they fail, they take an Elephant Round sniper shot to the chest.

    • Alternately, shooting down the A/V-4 will also do the trick, if someone in the party brought along a rocket launcher.

  • If Nora isn't broadcasting, Militech will check the bunker before pursuing the party, giving them a chance to get away - see above, but they only have the opportunity to fail the stealth check twice instead of thrice.

Lights Out (Maelstrom Version)

There's a dull pounding coming from the front doors. If it were 2025, they'd be unbreakable to most forces - but it's 2045, and Maelstrom aren't exactly "most forces."

You hear crumbling rock and shrieking steel, along with an angry roar from the other side of the doors - whatever nightmare Maelstrom has dug up, apparently they finally found purchase enough to rip one of the doors to the side. In the light, you see dark shapes, arguing about who goes first. The psycho that pulled the door off its hinges turns and decapitates one of the arguing goons, sending the rest scurrying towards the doors - evidently, they want whatever's in the bunker just as much as you do.

Roll for initiative.

Maelstrom doesn't give a damn about the party, except that they may have killed Maelstrom members, and will instead seek to get into and loot the bunker instead of pursuing the party back into the ruins. They do this regardless of if Nora is broadcasting or not.

This is a good opportunity to make an enemy of the psycho, but Maelstrom definitely lets the party off easier than Militech.

Live Broadcast From The Old Combat Zone

At this point, if Nightlight's construct has been rescued (and understands the situation), she senses a good time for good publicity - everyone loves seeing Maelstrom get wacked, and Militech isn't supposed to be in Night City at all, after all. She'll ask the party to hook her back up to her broadcasting systems and help her get the word out.

  • If the party wishes to do so, it involves pulling back a panel in the wall next to Nora's corpse/netrunning chair and inserting Nightlight's construct into a computer system found there.

  • From there, players will need to locate the damaged hardlines that formerly connected her to the net and repair them - a DV21 Basic Tech check, lowered to DV17 if two actions are taken instead of one.

  • Once they've made the repair, Nightlight's voice begins transmitting through an old FM radio somewhere in the kitchen - she's back on the air, and asks to be inserted into an Agent (also an action).

  • From that point on, she's live and transmitting across her usual pirate wavelength, commentating her rescue like a wrestling match through cameras positioned around the bunker and hidden in the ruins outside.

If the party successfully rescues her, every surviving member gains a personal rep.

Once the party has rescued Nightlight successfully, proceed to the final beat of the campaign.

This beat takes place in the @UUID[Scene.aNH5f8HeCSS472Gr]{Afterlife, Sublevel 3.} Place the player tokens and Vido in the green room, switch to it, then read the following.

Unexpected Results

Back at the Afterlife, Vido looks at the device containing Nora Jackson's AI personality with a mixture of amusement and concern.

"Well… certainly not what I was expecting by any means, but we can still make this work. With you consent, mon cheri?"

After Nightlight indicates she'll allow the transfer, Vido walks to a holographic table in what was going to be the Afterlife's information center and broadcasting hub. Plugging Nightlight in, her head appears in the center of the table, floating and watching the party. She is, you suspect, recreating the face of Nora before her unfortunate accident, though her eyes are glowing and her hair has been braided with electrical cables.

"Ah…" she sighs. "That's some good tech. Good evening Afterlife, this is Nightlight, reporting live and in person from the greatest club this side of paradise." She smiles down at you - it's meant to be endearing, you're sure, but the giant holographic AI head is definitely giving Overlord vibes, whether she intends to or not. "Thanks for the rescue, kiddos. And for the sponsor," she adds, looking at Vido. "Sorry I can't attend the party in person, but maybe that's for the best. You kids have fun."

Her head vanishes as she retreats into the Afterlife's computer systems, presumably to do… whatever it is AIs do when they're not trying to take over the world or murder the meatbags.

"Well then," Vido continues - his face is still a mix of concerned bemusement. "I suppose we should go inform the Owner that the Afterlife's new resident will not be requiring a hotel room, non? And then, there is a party to attend - come now, it's time to celebrate your achievements!"

Mission complete - assuming Nightlight was rescued successfully, the party receives their pay. Additionally, the party Vido is referring to is being thrown at least in part as a celebration of the party's efforts - between the toasts and Nightlight's broadcast, each party member gains an additional personal rep.

The conclusion of this mission also unlocks the final service of the Afterlife - see @UUID[JournalEntry.3Ke6zuK7DdKXvMp7]{11. Pirate Radio} for details.

You Have Now Completed Ticket To The Afterlife - Congratulations!

I hope you've enjoyed the campaign, but your journey in Night City doesn't have to be over! The intent of Ticket To The Afterlife has always been to provide a prospective new party with a foundation and starting point for their own adventures, whatever those might be, and to give new GMs some experience running games, to see what goes into making a mission work.

And my own work isn't over, either!

If you're interested in continuing to expand the Afterlife yourself, reach out! I can be found on the Foundry RED Discord server as Prometheus, or on Reddit at /u/TheInvaderZim. The module originally had considerable sidequest support planned, but the scope of the project got away from me and I intend to move on - but I'd love to help package your content and make it a part of the bigger whole!

If you have thoughts or feedback on the campaign, as ever, please take the time to fill out the feedback survey for this campaign, located here. It should only take 5 minutes, and will be actively considered as I make revisions to the campaign down the line - just because its missions are complete, doesn't mean I won't be making revisions, down the road!

Thank you so much for playing - I have more content in the pipeline, including a globe-trotting treasure-hunt, so stay tuned!

  • Prometheus.
